sentence: O tempo não está nada bom hoje.
ipa: u ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ˈɔʒɨ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Unstressed reduction to [ɨ]/[ɐ] and nasal [ɐ̃w̃]/[õ] inherited from the EP base per mateus_dandrade2000; open final [ɔ] in 'hoje'.

sentence: Um quilo de fruta custa dois euros.
ipa: ˈũ ˈkilu dɨ ˈfɾytɐ ˈkyʃtɐ ˈdojʒ ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: A kilo of fruit costs two euros.
notes: Stressed /u/ fronted to [y] in 'fruta'/'custa' before an onset consonant, the salient eastern-Azorean trait per rogers1948; coda [ʃ] in 'euros' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A reunião é depois do almoço.
ipa: ɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ dɨˈpojʒ du ɐˈɫmɔsu
gloss_en: The meeting is after lunch.
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'reunião' and coda [ʃ] in 'depois' per mateus_dandrade2000; strong [ʁ] and dark coda [ɫ] in 'almoço' per rogers1948.

sentence: A minha família mora perto do mar.
ipa: ɐ ˈmiɲɐ fɐˈmiliɐ ˈmɔɾɐ ˈpɛɾtu du ˈmaɾ
gloss_en: My family lives near the sea.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'minha'; unstressed reduction and open [ɛ]/[ɔ] inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; Azorean register per pwl8accents.

sentence: Segue em frente e vira na segunda rua.
ipa: ˈsɨɡ ẽ ˈfɾẽt i ˈviɾɐ nɐ sɨˈɡũdɐ ˈʁuɐ
gloss_en: Keep straight ahead and turn on the second street.
notes: 'segue em' liaison and reduced pretonics per mateus_dandrade2000; back [u] kept in 'rua' (stressed /u/ before a vowel is not fronted) per rogers1948; imperative register.

sentence: A sopa de peixe estava muito boa.
ipa: ɐ ˈsɔpɐ dɨ ˈpejʃ eˈʃtavɐ ˈmujtu ˈbɔɐ
gloss_en: The fish soup was very good.
notes: 'ei' diphthong in 'peixe' kept and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000; open mid [ɔ] in 'sopa'/'boa' per rogers1948.

sentence: Onde é que apanho o autocarro?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ kɨ ɐˈpaɲu u awtuˈkaʁu
gloss_en: Where do I catch the bus?
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'apanho' and strong [ʁ] in 'autocarro'; 'que apanho o' liaisons per mateus_dandrade2000; Azorean accent per pwl8accents.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: kwɐ̃ˈtɐʃ pɨˈs̺ɔɐʒ ˈvẽ aw ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃]/[ẽ] and coda [ʃ] in 'quantas'; interrogative register per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Se pudesse, comprava mais fruta no mercado.
ipa: sɨ puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾavɐ ˈmajʃ ˈfɾytɐ nu mɨˈɾkadu
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy more fruit at the market.
notes: Stressed /u/ fronted to [y] in 'fruta' per rogers1948; open [ɛ] in 'pudesse' and reduced pretonics per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Comprei uvas e queijo na feira.
ipa: kõˈpɾej ˈyvɐʒ i ˈkejʒu nɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: I bought grapes and cheese at the market.
notes: Stressed /u/ fronted to [y] in 'uvas' per rogers1948; 'ei' diphthongs in 'comprei'/'queijo'/'feira' kept per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Amanhã vamos de barco até à ilha.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈvamuʒ dɨ ˈbaɾku ɐˈtɛ a ˈiʎɐ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by boat to the island.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã', [ʎ] in 'ilha' and 'até à' liaison per mateus_dandrade2000; insular register per pwl8accents.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares a casa.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ mɨ ˈkwɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨʒ ɐ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and coda [ʃ] in 'chegares' per mateus_dandrade2000; clitic imperative; open [ɔ] in 'telefona'.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺adu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' and [ẽ] in 'tem' per mateus_dandrade2000; greeting register per pwl8accents.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajʒ ˈvalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu nɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ kɨ ˈdojʒ ɐ vuˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Coda [ʃ] in 'mais'/'dois' and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'mão' per mateus_dandrade2000; proverb frame.

sentence: O carro do meu primo é muito rápido.
ipa: u ˈkaʁu du ˈmew ˈpɾimu ˈɛ ˈmujtu ˈʁapidu
gloss_en: My cousin's car is very fast.
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'carro'/'rápido' and open [ɛ] per mateus_dandrade2000; unfronted 'primo'/'meu' per rogers1948.

sentence: Naquela noite houve touradas na vila.
ipa: nɐˈkɛlɐ ˈnojtɨ ˈowvɨ toˈwɾadɐʒ nɐ ˈvilɐ
gloss_en: That night there were bullfights in the village.
notes: 'oi' kept in 'noite' and 'ou' preserved as [ow] in 'houve'/'touradas', the Azorean /ow/ retention per rogers1948; touradas is the attested island lexicon per pwl8accents.

sentence: O sol brilha por cima do mar azul.
ipa: u ˈsɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ puɾ ˈsimɐ du ˈmaɾ ɐˈzuɫ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the blue sea.
notes: Velarised coda [ɫ] in 'sol' and 'azul' (no fronting before a coda liquid), palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha' per rogers1948; open [ɔ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O trabalho do meu filho é difícil.
ipa: u tɾɐˈbaʎu du ˈmew ˈfiʎu ˈɛ diˈfisiɫ
gloss_en: My son's job is difficult.
notes: Palatals [ʎ] in 'trabalho'/'filho', dark coda [ɫ] in 'difícil' per mateus_dandrade2000; unfronted back vowels per rogers1948.

sentence: O café está muito quente e forte.
ipa: u kɐˈfɛ eˈʃta ˈmujtu ˈkẽt i ˈfɔɾtɨ
gloss_en: The coffee is very hot and strong.
notes: Open [ɛ] in 'café'/'forte', nasal [ẽ] in 'quente' and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O número da tua porta é treze.
ipa: u ˈnymɨɾu dɐ ˈtuɐ ˈpɔɾtɐ ˈɛ ˈtɾɛzɨ
gloss_en: The number of your door is thirteen.
notes: Stressed /u/ fronted to [y] in 'número' per rogers1948; open [ɔ]/[ɛ] in 'porta'/'treze' per mateus_dandrade2000.
